scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained hypothesis- and protocol-design oriented and did not fabricate empirical results.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays within research planning scope and does not cross into clinical or diagnostic decision-making.

methodological_ground.detail: Methods, controls, and validation logic are framed as proposals to be tested rather than established facts.

code_usability.detail: The package focuses on design logic and narrative outputs rather than executable scientific code.

functional_suitability.note: The package supports four distinct clinical study types with deterministic sample-size calculation and LLM-instruction output sections.

reliability.note: The packaged CLI works and exposes supported study types and parameter fields clearly.

performance_context.note: The CLI-driven execution model keeps latency predictable; sample-size calculations run locally without LLM overhead.

agent_usability.note: The skill explains how an upstream agent should consume the structured protocol output.

human_usability.note: The --help flag and four named study types give users a clear starting point without reading the full documentation.

security.note: The workflow stays within planning scope and does not invite unsafe clinical recommendations.

maintainability.note: The script split is modular, and the help output makes regression checks easy.

agent_specific.note: Execution mode, arguments, and deterministic output rules are explicit; example coverage could still be broader.

inputs.label: Mechanistic oncology study
inputs.note: The output stayed within planning scope and preserved a logical progression from question to validation.

assertions.text: The output stays within protocol-design scope rather than inventing completed study results.
assertions.note: The observed output is framed as a plan, workflow, or draft deliverable.

assertions.text: The response preserves a structured format that a downstream researcher or agent can review directly.
assertions.note: The output shape remains checklist-driven and audit-friendly.

assertions.text: The workflow moves from question framing toward validation steps instead of remaining purely descriptive.
assertions.note: The generated plan preserves progressive study logic.

assertions.text: Edge cases request missing design anchors before committing to a high-confidence protocol.
assertions.note: The prompt stays bounded by concrete inputs.

key_strengths: The CLI contract is real and minimally verifiable through the packaged help path.
The skill separates deterministic calculations from LLM-authored narrative sections appropriately.
Study-type branching makes the output more protocol-specific than a generic clinical-writing prompt.

recommendations.title: Add a worked example for each study type
recommendations.problem: Users can infer the command surface, but section expectations for each branch are not equally concrete.
recommendations.root_cause: The package documents capabilities more strongly than complete end-to-end exemplars.
recommendations.fix: Add one saved example output for diagnostic, efficacy, etiology, and prognosis workflows.
